title: ACH Origination Anomalies
spl:
index=payments sourcetype="ach:origination" sec_code="WEB"
| stats sum(amount) as batch_total, dc(individual_id) as distinct_receivers by company_id, file_id
| eventstats avg(batch_total) as avg_batch by company_id
| where batch_total > 3*avg_batch OR distinct_receivers > 500
| sort -batch_total
description: Surfaces unusual ACH batches (debit concentration, new originators) that may indicate fraud or operational error.
value: Surfaces unusual ACH batches (debit concentration, new originators) that may indicate fraud or operational error.
implementation: Validate company_id against registered originators; alert on new SEC codes or return spikes after origination.
visualization: Table (suspicious batches), Line chart (ACH volume by company), Bar chart (returns vs. originations).
knownFalsePositives: ACH spikes from payroll, vendor batch files, or same-day settlement windows; align cutoffs and file schedules with treasury.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ACH/NACHA ingestion, treasury workstation.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="ach:origination"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate company_id against registered originators; alert on new SEC codes or return spikes after origination.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="ach:origination" sec_code="WEB"
| stats sum(amount) as batch_total, dc(individual_id) as distinct_receivers by company_id, file_id
| eventstats avg(batch_total) as avg_batch by company_id
| where batch_total > 3*avg_batch OR distinct_receivers > 500
| sort -batch_total
```

Understanding this SPL

**ACH Origination Anomalies** — Surfaces unusual ACH batches (debit concentration, new originators) that may indicate fraud or operational error.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="ach:origination"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ACH/NACHA ingestion, treasury workstation. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: ach:origination. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="ach:origination". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by company_id, file_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by company_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where batch_total > 3*avg_batch OR distinct_receivers > 500` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (suspicious batches), Line chart (ACH volume by company), Bar chart (returns vs. originations).
grandmaExplanation: In Financial Services, we pay close attention to "ACH Origination Anomalies" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
